perturbation.original_text: Customer may not cancel a previously accepted Purchase Order .
perturbation.changed_text: Customer may cancel a previously accepted Purchase Order subject to a 5% cancellation fee.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding cancellation of Purchase Orders. The original text states that Customer cannot cancel, while the modification allows cancellation with a fee. This creates ambiguity if Customer attempts to cancel an order, leading to disputes over the enforceability of the initial no-cancellation clause versus the new fee-based cancellation right. The contradicted_location is clause 4.3 where Philips rights in case of order cancellation is being reffered, thus making a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Purchase Order shall be deemed to be accepted by Philips until accepted in writing (including by email or another agreed manner of communication) by Philips or as otherwise contemplated by clause 4.4 below. Philips shall not reject any Purchase Order which is placed in accordance with the Forecast, the Lead Time and otherwise in accordance with this Agreement, unless Philips is entitled to reject on the basis of clause 3.1 or 4.4.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited cancellation of a Purchase Order. The modified text allows cancellation subject to a 5% fee. This directly contradicts the implication of the original "Customer may not cancel" clause, creating a direct in-text contradiction, and affects Philips rights according to the contradicted clause.

perturbation.original_text: Prices are firm and fixed for the Term and shall include all costs for work performed, Delivery according to this Agreement and packaging as set forth in clause 5.3 above.
perturbation.changed_text: Prices are firm and fixed for the initial two years of the Term and shall include all costs for work performed, Delivery according to this Agreement and packaging as set forth in clause 5.3 above. After two years, prices may be adjusted annually based on market conditions.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating prices are fixed for the Term initially, but then allowing for adjustments after two years. This directly conflicts with the 'firm and fixed for the Term' statement, creating uncertainty on the applicability of the pricing terms. The contradicting definition of 'Term' at clause 1. DEFINITIONS where 'Term' has the meaning ascribed thereto in clause 12.1, makes this change create an ambiguity and in-text contradiction.

perturbation.justification: The original text states that prices are 'firm and fixed for the Term', while the changed text limits this to the initial two years and allows for adjustments thereafter. This directly contradicts the original fixed pricing commitment for the entire Term. Additionally, this creates an ambiguity as the contradicted text clause 1. DEFINITIONS where 'Term' has the meaning ascribed thereto in clause 12.1, the duration is now uncertain regarding pricing terms, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Philips will bear all costs, including transportation and labor costs, in connection with the repair or replacement of, and all other costs or damages Customer may incur as a result of Products not complying with clause 7.1.
perturbation.changed_text: Philips will bear the transportation costs in connection with the repair or replacement of Products not complying with clause 7.1. Labor costs will be the responsibility of the Customer.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction concerning responsibility for labor costs related to repairs. The original states Philips covers all costs, including labor, while the modification shifts labor costs to Customer. This contradiction is directly present in clause 7.6, where the original text states "Philips will bear all costs", but the modified text only accounts for transportation costs. It affects the Customer's financial obligations and warranty rights, generating a situation of contractual interpretation issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Philips will bear the transportation costs in connection with the repair or replacement of Products not complying with clause 7.1. Labor costs will be the responsibility of the Customer. If Philips agrees that Customer performs the repair, Philips will provide Customer free of charge with any replacement Product or upgrade necessary, and will reimburse Customer for all costs relating to such repair, including any related labor costs.
perturbation.justification: The original text states Philips bears all costs, including labor, while the changed text assigns labor costs to the Customer. The contradicted text also now includes both contradictory terms, stating that Philips will bear transportation costs, but the Customer will bear labor costs, but then stating that Philips will reimburse the Customer for all costs, including labor costs. This contradiction is a clear conflict within clause 7.6 and affects the Customer's financial obligations, directly falling under the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.
